findings.detail: At the skill-spec rate of 230 wpm, reading time is 5.90 min — marginally below the 6-min floor by ~6 seconds. At the artifact-mix.json canonical rate of 200 wpm, reading time is 6.79 min, squarely within [6, 8]. Word count 1358 is within [1200, 1600]. The discrepancy is negligible.
findings.fix: No action required. If a future revision trims the word count significantly below 1200, revisit.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title. No YAML frontmatter. reading.meta.json present and parses. All six required sections present in correct order.

length_budget.notes: Word count 1358 within [1200, 1600]. Soft finding: at 230 wpm (skill spec formula) time is 5.90 min vs 6.0 floor; passes at 200 wpm (artifact-mix canonical). No hard fail.

citations.notes: Primary resource [1] cited multiple times across all sections. Supporting resources [2] and [3] cited throughout. Bibliography entries match all inline markers exactly.

no_forward_references.notes: All concepts are from this topic or prior topics (12.1 functions; 11.x variables, data types, for loops). Mention-and-defer at line 34 ('keyword arguments, default values') is a single one-sentence named pointer with no definition or bolding — compliant with scope_discipline.

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace to corpus: parameter/argument distinction (§5.1.1), vending machine analogy (§5.1.1), positional matching (§5.1.3), return exits immediately (§5.2.2), None semantics (§5.2.4), print-vs-return rule (§5.2.5), spec-first discipline (§6 Step 1), find_highest None-flag pattern (§7 Pattern 1), function chaining (§7 Pattern 2), three usage patterns for returned values (§5.2.3).

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined on first use with bold-term/plain-meaning shape. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms at once. Concrete analogies (calculator, vending machine) precede formal definitions. Sentences are short and direct; no hedging phrases detected.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences. Worked Example uses numbered steps (Step 1-7). Inline code examples appear within Key Concepts at each concept introduction. Two comparison tables break up prose effectively. Definitions use bold-term/plain-meaning shape throughout.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights appear in the reading.

scope.notes: Content stays within headline scope (parameters, arguments, positional parameters, return values, return keyword, implicit None, print vs return). Keyword arguments and default values named-and-deferred in one sentence without definition — compliant. len() and str() used as inline contextual code tools only, not taught as standalone concepts.

summary: Reading passes all checks: correct format and section structure, word count within target, all primary and supporting resources cited, no forward references, all sampled assertions faithful to corpus, beginner-readable with concrete-first examples throughout, well-formatted with numbered steps and inline code, no scope creep; one negligible soft finding on the 230 wpm reading-time floor.
